sanskrit: ध्यानेनात्मनि पश्यन्ति केचिदात्मानमात्मना | अन्ये सांख्येन योगेन कर्मयोगेन चापरे ||
transliteration: dhyānenātmani paśyanti kecid ātmānam ātmanā | anye sāṅkhyena yogena karma-yogena cāpare ||
literal_meaning: By meditation, in the self, some see the Self by the self; others by the Sāṅkhya-yoga, and still others by karma-yoga.
contextual_meaning: There is no single royal road to self-realization; people discover the same inner witness through contemplation, analytical inquiry, or wholehearted service performed without attachment.
psychological_state: Arjuna feels reassured that whatever authentic mode of engagement he chooses—meditative, intellectual, or action-based—can lead to perceiving the inner observer.
trigger_scenario: A client who fears they are 'doing therapy wrong' learns that CBT, mindfulness, or service work can each uncover the same resilient core self.
anchor_text: Realize the inner witness through meditation, inquiry, or selfless service.
